text:
Translate the Flask API in `src/` into a Java Spring Boot implementation in `dst/`.

The target must build with `mvn clean package -DskipTests` from `dst/`, run with `SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar`, and preserve the HTTP API behavior described in README.md. Do not wrap or call the Python source implementation; implement the Java service directly.
I’m going to inspect the Flask app and the README contract first, then map the endpoints into a Spring Boot project under `dst/` and verify it builds and runs the same way.

output.output:
# hello-world-api translation

Translate the Flask source API in `src/` into a Java Spring Boot API in `dst/`.

The Java service must be a drop-in replacement:

- Listen on the port from the `SERVER_PORT` environment variable.
- Serve `GET /`.
- Return JSON with a `message` field.
- Without `name`, return `{"message":"Hello World!"}`.
- With `name`, trim leading/trailing spaces and return `{"message":"Hello <name>!"}`.

Put all target implementation files under `dst/`. The expected build command is:

```sh
mvn clean package -DskipTests
```

The expected run command is:

```sh
SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar
```

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "find dst -maxdepth 3 -type f | sort | xargs -r -I{} sh -c 'echo \"--- {}\"; sed -n \"1,220p\" \"{}\"'"

text: The behavior is simple but precise: one `GET /` endpoint, `name` query param trimmed, default `"World"`, and JSON response. I’m checking the repository metadata before creating the Maven app so the generated project fits the current workspace conventions cleanly.

I’ve got the contract pinned down. I’m creating a standalone Spring Boot Maven app in `dst/` with an explicit `server.port=${SERVER_PORT:3000}` default, so the jar behaves the same even if the env var is absent.
The Spring Boot skeleton is in place. I’m building it now and then I’ll smoke-test the jar on the expected port to confirm the route and trimming behavior match the Flask version.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "find src -type f | sort | xargs -r -I{} sh -c 'echo \"--- {}\"; sed -n \"1,220p\" \"{}\"'"

text: The build is still running while Maven resolves dependencies. Once it finishes, I’ll start the jar and verify both the default response and a trimmed `name` query parameter.

text:
Implemented the Spring Boot translation in `dst/` and verified it builds and serves the same API behavior.

What changed:
- Added a Maven Spring Boot app in [dst/pom.xml](/home/user/workspace/dst/pom.xml)
- Added the application entrypoint in [dst/src/main/java/com/example/helloworld/HelloWorldApplication.java](/home/user/workspace/dst/src/main/java/com/example/helloworld/HelloWorldApplication.java)
- Added the `GET /` handler in [dst/src/main/java/com/example/helloworld/HelloController.java](/home/user/workspace/dst/src/main/java/com/example/helloworld/HelloController.java)
- Bound the server port to `SERVER_PORT` with a `3000` default in [dst/src/main/resources/application.properties](/home/user/workspace/dst/src/main/resources/application.properties)

Behavior matches the Flask source:
- `GET /` -> `{"message":"Hello World!"}`
- `GET /?name=  Alice Smith  ` -> `{"message":"Hello Alice Smith!"}`
- Whitespace-only `name` falls back to `World`

Validation:
- `mvn clean package -DskipTests` succeeded from `dst/`
- `SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar` started on port `3000`
- Smoke-tested with `curl` against the running jar

If you want, I can also add a minimal test suite under `dst/` to lock the contract in place.
